title: Lilaeopsis
taxonomy: Lilaeopsis belongs to the family of aquatic plants known as Haloragaceae. It is a genus of small, delicate plants commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Lilaeopsis is typically classified as a type of flowering plant, with its species often being referred to as 'dwarf chain ferns' or 'micro sword ferns' due to their compact growth habit. The genus Lilaeopsis has several species, with some of the most well-known being Lilaeopsis novae-zelandiae and Lilaeopsis brasiliensis. These species are often used in aquariums and aquatic gardens due to their unique and attractive foliage.

side_effects: Lilaeopsis, when used as a food source, can cause gastrointestinal upset in some individuals due to its high fiber content. The consumption of large amounts of Lilaeopsis may lead to bloating, gas, and stomach cramps. Additionally, its high oxalate levels may cause kidney stones or worsen existing kidney problems in susceptible individuals. However, the side effects of Lilaeopsis are generally rare and more commonly associated with excessive consumption.
